questions.question: Which leader made Ghana the first sub-Saharan African colony to achieve independence from European rule in 1957?
questions.explanation: Kwame Nkrumah led Ghana (formerly Gold Coast) to independence on March 6, 1957. He was a pan-African visionary. Overthrown in a 1966 military coup while visiting China.
questions.memory_hook: Kwame Nkrumah: Ghana independence 1957 — first sub-Saharan African colony to be free. Overthrown 1966 in China.

questions.question: Earth's mantle makes up what percentage of Earth's volume, and what drives tectonic plate movement?
options: 84% of volume; convection currents in the mantle
50% of volume; the core's magnetic field
84% of volume; rotation of the Earth
questions.answer: 84% of volume; convection currents in the mantle
questions.explanation: Earth's mantle (84% of Earth's volume) is solid rock that flows slowly over millions of years via convection currents — hotter material rises, cooler material sinks. This drives plate tectonics.
questions.memory_hook: Mantle: 84% of Earth's volume. Convection currents drive plate tectonics. Solid but flows very slowly.

questions.question: Lionel Messi won his record 8th Ballon d'Or after which tournament victory?
questions.explanation: Messi won his 8th Ballon d'Or following Argentina's 2022 World Cup victory — widely considered the greatest World Cup final ever, with France beaten in a dramatic penalty shootout after a 3-3 draw.
questions.memory_hook: Messi: 8th Ballon d'Or after 2022 World Cup with Argentina. 3-3 vs France, won on penalties.

questions.question: Greta Gerwig's 'Barbie' (2023) starring Margot Robbie grossed how much — making it the highest-grossing film directed by a woman?
questions.explanation: Barbie (2023) grossed $1.44 billion worldwide. Directed by Greta Gerwig. Also starred Ryan Gosling as Ken. The film sparked shortages of pink paint worldwide and widespread cultural conversation about feminism.
questions.memory_hook: Barbie 2023: $1.44B, highest-grossing film by a woman director. Greta Gerwig, Margot Robbie, Ryan Gosling.

questions.question: Monaco is the world's second-smallest country (after Vatican City). What is its area, and why do wealthy people live there?
questions.explanation: Monaco (2.02 km²) has no income tax, making it attractive to wealthy residents. Most densely populated sovereign state at ~26,000 people/km². Monte Carlo Casino was founded 1863. Prince Albert II rules today.
questions.memory_hook: Monaco: 2.02 km², no income tax. World's most densely populated country. Monte Carlo Casino founded 1863.

questions.question: The Marshall Plan was announced in 1947 and provided how much U.S. aid to rebuild Western Europe?
questions.explanation: Secretary of State George Marshall announced the European Recovery Program at Harvard on June 5, 1947. $13 billion was authorized over four years. It prevented communist takeovers in war-devastated Western Europe. Marshall won the Nobel Peace Prize 1953.
questions.memory_hook: Marshall Plan: $13B (1948-52). Announced at Harvard June 5, 1947. Marshall won Nobel 1953.

questions.question: Instagram was founded in 2010 and acquired by Facebook for how much, just 18 months after launching?
questions.explanation: Instagram launched October 6, 2010, with its square photo format and filter system. Facebook acquired it for $1 billion in April 2012 — considered an incredible bargain in retrospect. Now has 2 billion+ monthly users.
questions.memory_hook: Instagram: Oct 2010 launch. Acquired by Facebook for $1B in 2012 — now worth astronomically more.

questions.question: How does mass differ from weight, and how does your weight change on the Moon?
options: Mass is constant; weight = mass × gravity. On Moon: same mass, 1/6 the weight
Mass changes with gravity; weight stays constant
Mass doubles on the Moon; weight stays the same
questions.answer: Mass is constant; weight = mass × gravity. On Moon: same mass, 1/6 the weight
questions.explanation: Mass (kg) is constant anywhere. Weight (Newtons) = mass × gravitational acceleration. The Moon's gravity is ~1/6 of Earth's, so your weight on the Moon is 1/6 what it is on Earth — but your mass is identical.
questions.memory_hook: Mass = constant. Weight = mass × gravity. Moon: same mass, 1/6 the weight. W=mg.

questions.question: Oslo, Norway's capital, has had which other names in its history?
options: Always been called Oslo since Viking times
questions.explanation: Oslo was rebuilt and renamed Christiania by King Christian IV after a fire in 1624. Spelling changed to Kristiania in 1877. The city reverted to Oslo in 1925. Vigeland Sculpture Park (free, 24 hours) is the world's largest single-artist sculpture park.
questions.memory_hook: Oslo: named Christiania 1624, Kristiania 1877, back to Oslo 1925. Vigeland Park = world's largest single-artist sculpture park.

questions.question: Tinker v. Des Moines (1969) established what landmark principle about students' rights?
options: Students don't shed their constitutional rights at the schoolhouse gate
Students have no First Amendment rights during class
Teachers can restrict any speech they deem disruptive
questions.answer: Students don't shed their constitutional rights at the schoolhouse gate
questions.explanation: Tinker v. Des Moines (1969) arose when Iowa students wore black armbands protesting Vietnam. The Supreme Court ruled 7-2 for the students. Justice Abe Fortas wrote: 'Students do not shed their constitutional rights at the schoolhouse gate.'
questions.memory_hook: Tinker v. Des Moines 1969: armband protest won. 'Don't shed rights at the schoolhouse gate.' First Amendment for students.

questions.question: Elton John and Bernie Taupin: who writes the words and who writes the music?
options: Taupin writes words; Elton John sets them to music (often in under an hour)
Elton John writes words and music; Taupin produces
Both collaborate equally on each song
Taupin writes music; Elton writes words
questions.answer: Taupin writes words; Elton John sets them to music (often in under an hour)
questions.explanation: Taupin sends lyrics to Elton, who then composes music — often in under an hour. This unique long-distance collaboration has lasted 50+ years. Elton John sold 300M records and holds the record for fastest-selling single ('Candle in the Wind' 1997: 33M copies in 37 days).
questions.memory_hook: Elton + Taupin: Taupin writes words, Elton sets music. 300M records. 'Candle in the Wind' 1997 = fastest-selling single ever.

questions.question: Wimbledon is the only Grand Slam played on grass. When was it first held, and how much strawberries are consumed per tournament?
options: First held 1877; about 23 tonnes of strawberries consumed per tournament
questions.answer: First held 1877; about 23 tonnes of strawberries consumed per tournament
questions.explanation: Wimbledon is the oldest Grand Slam, first held in 1877. Strawberries and cream is the signature tradition — about 23 tonnes (with 7,000 liters of cream) consumed per tournament.
questions.memory_hook: Wimbledon: oldest Grand Slam (1877). Grass courts. All-white clothing. 23 tonnes of strawberries per tournament.
